# Fix Breaking Hook API Signature in PocketBase

## Background

You have a custom Go backend embedding PocketBase. The project was recently upgraded to PocketBase v0.31.0, but the custom event hooks are failing to compile and run because they use the older v0.22 API signature. In PocketBase v0.23.0+, there was a major API overhaul affecting hook registration and event propagation.

## Requirements

* Update `main.go` to fix the `OnRecordBeforeCreateRequest` hook for the `posts` collection.
* The hook must use the new `.BindFunc()` registration method.
* The hook handler must accept the new event type (`*core.RecordRequestEvent`).
* The hook handler must check if the `title` is empty. If it is, return a bad request error.
* The hook handler must generate a slug from the title using `core.Slugify(title)` and set it on the record's `slug` field.
* The hook handler MUST call and return `e.Next()` to properly propagate the execution chain. If omitted, the record creation will be blocked.
* The application must compile successfully and run.

## Implementation Hints

* Review the PocketBase v0.23+ upgrade guide for Go hooks.
* Hook registration changed from `.Add()` to `.BindFunc()`.
* Event handlers now receive a generic `*core.RecordRequestEvent` instead of specific event types.
* Handlers must return `e.Next()` to continue the middleware chain.
* Use `e.BadRequestError("Title cannot be empty", nil)` for validation errors instead of standard `fmt.Errorf`.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Start command: go run main.go serve --http="0.0.0.0:8090"
* Port: 8090
* API Endpoints:
  * POST `/api/collections/posts/records`: Accepts a JSON body with a `title` and creates a record with an auto-generated `slug`. Returns 200 OK.

**Updating Hook Signature**

I'm currently focused on rectifying the outdated hook API signature within the PocketBase project located at `/home/user/myproject`. The primary task involves migrating from the v0.22 API signature to the more recent v0.31.0 standard to ensure compatibility and optimal functionality.

**Refining Hook Implementation**

I'm now deep in the code, specifically working on the `main.go` file within the `/home/user/myproject` directory. My objective is to adjust the `OnRecordBeforeCreateRequest` hook for the `posts` collection to align with the v0.31.0 PocketBase API. This involves using `.BindFunc()` and ensuring proper error handling and slug generation as per the updated signature.

**Updating main.go**

I'm refining the event handling for the "posts" record. Specifically, I'm adjusting the `OnRecordBeforeCreateRequest` to `OnRecordCreateRequest` and updating the event handler signature to `core.RecordRequestEvent` for better alignment with the framework's conventions.

**Refining Post Hooks**

I'm updating the `main.go` to use `OnRecordCreateRequest` instead of `OnRecordBeforeCreateRequest` for the "posts" collection, aligning with PocketBase v0.23+ changes and adjusting the event handler signature to `core.RecordRequestEvent`.

**Investigating hook.Event Methods**

I'm currently examining the methods available on `hook.Event`, specifically focusing on the `Next()` function. My aim is to confirm its presence and understand its functionality within the broader context of event handling.

**Refining Event Handling**

I'm currently focusing on the implementation details of the `OnRecord CreateRequest` handler. My thoughts are centered on ensuring robust error handling, specifically for cases where the `title` field might be missing from the incoming request. This ensures graceful failure and clear feedback.

**Exploring RequestEvent Methods**

I'm reviewing the available methods on `core.RecordRequestEvent`. I need to confirm if `BadRequestError` is directly accessible on this type, as my current thoughts suggest it might be. This is crucial for proper error propagation.

**Investigating Error Location**

I'm currently tracing where `BadRequestError` might originate, specifically within `router.Event`, `RequestEvent`, or the event object itself. My immediate focus is on the `router.Event` module for this check.

…

created: 2025-07-12, last updated: 2026-03-24, [Read more](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tiny-slugify?activeTab=versions)

- **🚀 Tiny**: Core is < 1kB, full API < 2kB
- **🌳 Tree-shakeable**: Import only what you need
- **🔒 No global state**: Safe for serverless and testing
- **⚡ Fast**: 2-3x faster than alternatives on large datasets

> import { slugify } from "tiny-slugify/core"; slugify("Hello World"); // → 'Hello-World' slugify("Café & Restaurant"); // → 'Cafe-Restaurant'

# Slug Generation with Rust 'slugify-core' #GitHub - Qiita
created: 2025-09-21, last updated: 2026-03-24, [Read more](https://qiita.com/yuip/items/acf9bd40820d9aa1790f)

"slugify-core" is a high-performance slug generation library built with Rust.
It provides functionality to safely convert Unicode strings into URL-friendly slugs and can be used from other programming languages through multi-language FFI bindings.
The library can be utilized for URL generation in web applications and CMS systems, filename normalization, database key standardization, and many other purposes.
...
. For use from C or other languages, it can be built as a shared library.
...
Currently, Rust native support and C/C++ FFI bindings are available. Future support for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, and Go is planned.
...
"slugify-core" is an extremely useful Rust library for modern application development. It offers high performance, safety, Unicode support, customizability, multi-language support, and practicality, making it a reliable component for various applications. It can be utilized across a wide range of fields including web application URL generation, CMS system permalink creation, safe file system naming, database identifier normalization, and API endpoint standardization.

# Slugify.Core
created: 2025-03-18, last updated: 2025-10-10, [Read more](https://nugetmusthaves.com/Package/Slugify.Core)

##### Install
`Install-Package Slugify.Core`
`dotnet add package Slugify.Core`
`paket add Slugify.Core`
```
Slugify.Core Download (Unzip the "nupkg" after downloading)
```
...
With default settings, you will get an hyphenized, lowercase, alphanumeric version of any string you please, with any diacritics removed and collapsed whitespace, collapsed dashes and trimmed whitespace.

# Media Slugify - Orchard Core Documentation
created: 2024-11-25, last updated: 2026-06-10, [Read more](https://docs.orchardcore.net/en/2.1/reference/modules/Media.Slugify/)

# Media Slugify ( OrchardCore.Media.Slugify ) ¶
The Media Slugify feature enables *slugifying* assets in the Media Library.
...
Folders and files can be *slugified* automatically by enabling the Media Slugify feature.
Doing this will rename the example above to `the-team-2020.jpg` in folder `images-docs`.
The URL would be `/media/images-docs/the-team-2020.jpg`.
Different files can have the same slug, making it impossible to upload both without renaming one (e.g. `The team (2020).jpg` and `The Team 2020.jpg`).
...
Enabling the Media Slugify feature will not rename existing folders and files, only new folders and files will be *slugified*.

# slugify package - github.com/mjadobson/pb-plugin-slug
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-06-15, [Read more](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/mjadobson/pb-plugin-slug)

### Slugify
Generate PocketBase slugs from one or more record fields.
The plugin combines configured input fields in order, normalizes the text to lowercase alphanumeric segments separated by hyphens, truncates the result to the configured length, and adds numeric suffixes when needed to keep slugs unique.
...
Build PocketBase with the plugin:
...
On startup the plugin creates a shared `_plugins` collection if it doesn't already exist.
Create or update the `slugify` row in `_plugins` with:
- `plugin_name`: `slugify`
- `enabled`: `true` or `false`
- `config`: JSON config
Example `config` value:
...
```
Then make sure each target collection has:
- the fields listed in `input_fields`
- a text or editor field matching `output_field`
- a single-column `UNIQUE` index on `output_field` (recommended with `WHERE output_field != ''`)
The plugin runs after successful record create and update operations.
...
The PocketBase collection name to watch.

# pocketbase/pocketbase: Open Source realtime backend in 1 file
created: 2022-07-05, last updated: 2026-05-30, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase)

PocketBase is an open source Go backend that includes:
- embedded database (*SQLite*) with **realtime subscriptions**
- built-in **files and users management**
- convenient **Admin dashboard UI**
- and simple **REST-ish API**
**For documentation and examples, please visit https://pocketbase.io/docs .**
...
### Use as a Go framework/toolkit
PocketBase is distributed as a regular Go library package which allows you to build
your own custom app specific business logic and still have a single portable executable at the end.

# Base collections - PocketBase - Mintlify
created: 2026-03-03, last updated: 2026-04-14, [Read more](https://mintlify.wiki/pocketbase/pocketbase/collections/base-collections)

```
collection := core.NewBaseCollection("posts")
...
You can optionally specify a custom collection ID:
```
collection := core.NewBaseCollection("posts", "pbc_custom_id")
...
```
package main
import (
"log"
"github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase"
"github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/core"
)
...
app := pocketbase.New()

# Two feature suggestions: Field "Instructions" and "slug" field · pocketbase pocketbase · Discussion #3241
created: 2023-08-31, last updated: 2025-05-19, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/discussions/3241)

- When creating a field on a collection, add support for a "description" or "instructions" for the field.
...
- Implement a new "slug" field that auto-generates a slug based on another field, e.g.
 ```
title
```
or maybe even multiple other fields.
It would need the ability to be overridden/editable.

# Example of using PocketBase (v0.11.3) as a framework
created: 2023-01-21, last updated: 2026-03-23, [Read more](https://gist.github.com/kaboc/c391c062a7371757da28f04c72cf8b5e)

|--|--|
| | |

# Introduction - Extending PocketBase - Docs
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-06-14, [Read more](https://pocketbase.io/docs/use-as-framework/)

One of the main features of PocketBase is that
**it can be used as a framework** which enables you to write your own custom app business logic in Go or JavaScript and still have a portable backend at the end.
**Choose Extend with Go if you are already familiar with the language or have the time to learn it.** As the primary PocketBase language, the Go APIs are better documented and you'll be able to integrate with any 3rd party Go library since you'll have more control over the application flow.
...
**Choose Extend with JavaScript if you don't intend to write too much custom code and want a quick way to explore the PocketBase capabilities.** The embedded JavaScript engine is a pluggable wrapper around the existing Go APIs, so most of the time the slight performance penalty will be negligible because it'll invoke the Go functions under the hood.

input.result:
I have updated `main.go` to fix the breaking hook API signature for PocketBase v0.31.0.

Here is a summary of the changes made:
- Changed the hook registration from `.OnRecordBeforeCreateRequest("posts").Add(...)` to `.OnRecordCreateRequest("posts").BindFunc(...)` to match the v0.23+ API.
- Updated the event handler parameter from `*core.RecordCreateEvent` to the new `*core.RecordRequestEvent` type.
- Updated the error handling to use `e.BadRequestError("Title cannot be empty", nil)` instead of `fmt.Errorf`.
- Added a custom `slugify` function to convert the title to a slug (since `core.Slugify` does not exist in `github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/core` in version 0.31.0).
- Set the `slug` field via `e.Record.Set("slug", slugify(title))`.
- Ensured that `e.Next()` is called and returned at the end of the handler to properly propagate the execution chain.

The application compiles perfectly and handles the API requests as expected.
